options: A: remove .python-version entirely (let uv resolve from pyproject requires-python >=3.11)
B: loosen to a floor like 3.11 or 3.12
C: keep a single pin but bump it to 3.14 to match active dev
payload.question: FR-001 .python-version loosening shape: which form should the new .python-version take so it stops blocking local agents on 3.14 while keeping CI predictable?

payload.final_answer: B: floor at 3.11. Replace .python-version contents with '3.11' so it aligns with pyproject.toml's requires-python = '>=3.11' and stops imposing a higher implicit contributor floor than packaging does. Avoids broken patch-level pin behavior on 3.13/3.14 dev environments while leaving the contributor minimum unchanged.
